items.text:
Hi Marisol,

Your spring parking permit is ready for pickup at the Transportation Office, Room 118, Grant Hall. Please bring your student ID and the $30 permit fee before Friday, March 6, at 4:30 PM. Permits not collected by then will be canceled.

Thanks,
Mr. Delaney

questions.stem: According to the email, what must the student provide when picking up the permit?

options.A: A campus map and a receipt
options.B: Proof of identity and the fee
options.C: A permit number and a deposit
options.D: A registration slip and a stipend

questions.explanation: The email states the student should bring a student ID and the $30 permit fee, which the correct option paraphrases as proof of identity and the fee.

questions.stem: In the email, the word "canceled" is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: In this context, a permit that is "canceled" is one that becomes invalid and can no longer be used or collected.

items.text: Notice: Campus Wi-Fi service will be suspended in Bellweather Hall on Sunday, April 19, from 2:00 AM until 6:00 AM for scheduled network upgrades performed by IT Services. Wired connections in the computer lab will remain active throughout. Students should save their work beforehand. Normal service resumes by 6:00 AM.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how long will the Wi-Fi outage last?

options.B: Roughly three hours in the evening

questions.explanation: The outage runs from 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM, which is about four hours before dawn.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students in the computer lab during the outage?

options.A: They must wait until IT Services reopens the building.
options.B: They can use wired connections in the lab.
options.C: They should switch to personal mobile data instead.
options.D: They will lose access until normal service resumes.

questions.explanation: Since wired connections in the computer lab remain active during the outage, it can be inferred that students there can still get online through those connections.

items.text:
Hi Owen,

Your replacement student ID card is ready for pickup at the One-Card Office, Room 220, Simmons Hall. Please bring a $10 fee and a valid photo ID. The office is open Tuesday through Saturday until 3:30 PM.

Thanks,
Ms. Whitfield

questions.stem: According to the email, how much is the fee for a replacement ID?
questions.explanation: The email states that a $10 fee is required, which the correct option restates as ten dollars for the replacement.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about someone who arrives to pick up the ID without a photo ID?

options.A: That person cannot finish the ID pickup process.
options.B: That person receives a temporary paper ID instead.
options.C: That person is charged an additional late fee.
options.D: That person must wait until the next business day.

questions.explanation: Since the email requires bringing a valid photo ID to pick up the replacement, someone without one would not be able to finish the process.

items.text: Notice: The heating system in Callahan Hall will be serviced Monday, February 9, through Wednesday, February 11. Classrooms may become uncomfortably cold during repairs. Portable heaters are available for a $15 deposit at the Facilities Office. Normal heating will be restored by February 12.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how much is the deposit for a portable heater?
questions.explanation: The notice states a $15 deposit is required for a portable heater, which matches fifteen dollars for the heater.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about classrooms in Callahan Hall during the repair period?

options.A: They will be closed during the repairs.
options.B: They will be relocated to another building.
options.C: They may feel colder than usual.
options.D: They will require an extra cleaning fee.

questions.explanation: Because the notice warns that classrooms may become uncomfortably cold during repairs, it can be inferred that they may feel colder than usual.
